_note: Fictional inbox for Marcus Holloway (p018), corporate trainee. Mix of client instructions, supervisor messages, internal admin, junk and knowledge alerts. Anchor 'today' is 2026-05-15. Threads share a threadId.

emails.subject: URGENT: Project Lighthouse - SPA mark-up needed by 9am tomorrow
emails.body: Marcus - the buyer's solicitors just sent the revised SPA. I need a clean mark-up of sections 6 (warranties) and 9 (limitations) by 9am. Track changes on. Don't worry about the schedules, Sarah is taking those. Call me if anything is unclear. G.

emails.subject: RE: URGENT: Project Lighthouse - SPA mark-up needed by 9am tomorrow
emails.body: On it. Two questions: (1) do you want me to flag where the buyer's wording differs from the AGNDA standard, or just respond on substance? (2) the limitation cap is back at 100% of consideration - do we want to push to 50% as discussed? I'll send a draft by 7am.

emails.subject: RE: URGENT: Project Lighthouse - SPA mark-up needed by 9am tomorrow
emails.body: 1) Flag it. 2) Push to 50% but accept 75% if they push back hard. Get sleep when you can. G.

emails.subject: Lighthouse - board pack for Friday's board meeting
emails.body: Can you put together the board pack for Friday? Cover: (i) status of negotiations, (ii) open commercial points, (iii) timetable to signing, (iv) DD red-flag log. There's a template in the Lighthouse folder under /Working/Templates. Send me a draft Wednesday EOD.

emails.subject: New instruction - Project Birch (Tessera bolt-on)
emails.body: Tessera have a new bolt-on for one of their portcos. Target is a UK SaaS business; sector overview attached separately. We need (a) Phase 0 intel by Tuesday, (b) draft DD scope by Thursday, (c) proposed team. Marcus please run Phase 0; Sarah will take scope. Speed matters but quality more.

emails.subject: RE: New instruction - Project Birch (Tessera bolt-on)
emails.body: On it. Confirming the target's full legal name and any group structure I should know about? I'll start with Companies House and the standard adverse media sweep.

emails.subject: FW: Conflict check - Project Birch
emails.body: Conflict check came back clear. Risk team flagged that we've previously acted for a competitor of the target on a separate matter (closed 2023). They're comfortable; just be aware. Marcus please confirm in your Phase 0 note.

emails.subject: Helix - draft board minutes for review
emails.body: Marcus - draft minutes from yesterday's Helix board attached for your review before they go to the chair. Particular focus on the option-pool resolution; I've left a comment for you. Need them out by Wednesday.

emails.subject: Westgate - 1954 Act brief, can you take this on?
emails.body: Marcus - Westgate has a 1954 Act renewal coming up that fits well with your real estate study. I know you're not in the seat but it's a discrete piece and partner-credit-friendly. Bea has approved. Are you free to pick this up next week? Minimal disruption to your corporate work.

emails.subject: Time entries due (Friday reminder)
emails.body: Friendly reminder: time entries for the week need to be in by 5pm today or we lose them on the Lighthouse fee estimate. You currently have 3 days unposted (Mon, Wed, Thu). Let me know if you want help with narratives.

emails.subject: Knowledge alert: ICO publishes new guidance on cookie consent
emails.body: FYI for the trainee group. ICO has published updated guidance on cookie consent platforms; particularly relevant if you're touching the Brindle breach matter or any Lattice instructions. Headline points: (i) accept-all and reject-all must be equally prominent, (ii) consent must be specific to processing purposes, (iii) cookie walls remain non-compliant. Full bulletin attached.

emails.subject: BD: Tessera roundtable - your thoughts on the invite list
emails.body: We're hosting a Tessera GP roundtable on 12 June. Theme: 'AI risks in the data room'. I want each of you to suggest two attendees from your own network - other lawyers, GCs, deal advisors. Push it wide. By Friday please.

emails.subject: Coffee Friday?
emails.body: If you're not chained to Lighthouse, want to grab a coffee Friday afternoon? I want to pick your brain about the seat rotation forms before I submit. Also they've got those salted caramel things downstairs again.

emails.subject: Oxford Street lease - landlord's draft attached
emails.body: First draft from landlord's solicitors attached. Long, full repairing, no break, RPI uplifts. Marcus - Bea wants you to do a redline summary as a stretch piece. Olivia is your point of contact. Targeting our mark-up by next Friday.

emails.subject: EXCLUSIVE: 50% off your Westlaw subscription - act before midnight
emails.body: Dear MARCUS, you have been selected for an EXCLUSIVE 50% OFF your firm Westlaw subscription. This offer expires at MIDNIGHT TONIGHT. Click here to redeem now: https://thomson-reuters-promo.example/redeem?id=8HX2

emails.subject: Scheduled maintenance: iManage and Outlook - Saturday 17 May
emails.body: iManage and Outlook will be unavailable from 22:00 on Saturday 17 May to 04:00 on Sunday 18 May for scheduled maintenance. Please save and close any open documents in iManage by 21:30 to avoid data loss. The firm VPN will remain available throughout.

emails.subject: Lattice - subprocessor list, are these all approved?
emails.body: Lattice's enterprise customer wants confirmation that every subprocessor is on the approved list before close. List attached. Ines will lead but Marcus please give it a first pass and flag anything that smells off.

emails.subject: RE: Lattice - subprocessor list, are these all approved?
emails.body: Three things flagged: (1) DataPilot Inc isn't on the approved list and the SCCs are 2010, (2) two of the analytics tools transfer to the US without an explicit transfer mechanism, (3) one of the support providers is a sub-sub-processor flowed down from Brimstone Cloud - not sure if that's already covered. Note for Ines.

emails.subject: RE: Lattice - subprocessor list, are these all approved?
emails.body: Good catches Marcus. (1) DataPilot is the deal-breaker; need new SCCs or a different vendor. (2) Will draft transfer impact assessments. (3) Brimstone flow-down does cover it but we should ask Lattice to update their public list. I'll take it from here.

emails.subject: Pemberton adjudication - draft response, urgent
emails.body: Marcus - I know you're not on this matter but I'm drowning. Could you draft the section on contemporaneous notices (paragraphs 41-58 of the referral)? Aaron has approved you helping. Templates in the matter file. Need a first draft by Monday morning.

emails.subject: Marston co-existence - their counter, please review
emails.body: Marcus - I know this is technically an IP matter but Nicola wants to give you exposure. Their counter to our co-existence draft is attached. Would you do a clause-by-clause comparison and flag anything that's drifted? Two-page summary by Friday.

emails.subject: Trainee seat rotation - your preferences (deadline 23 May)
emails.body: Time to nominate your preferences for the September rotation. Form attached. Three preferences in order. Reminder: international secondments require a separate application. Deadline 23 May, no extensions.

emails.subject: Reminder: Bundle for trainee induction - Monday 19 May
emails.body: Reminder that the trainee induction event is Monday 19 May, 09:00-17:00 in the Mortimer Room. Pre-reading bundle attached. You will be expected to have read sections 1-3 before the morning session.

emails.subject: FW: FW: FW: Quill Media - DSAR template needed urgently
emails.body: Forwarding for visibility - Quill have had another wave of co-ordinated DSARs (sixty-three since Monday). They want our standard response template adapted for their podcast division by tomorrow lunchtime. Ines please lead, Marcus please be available to support.

emails.subject: Helix Series C - investor side letter
emails.body: Investor's side letter attached. They want MFN, board observer, and a tag on any down-round. Two questions for you: (i) do these conflict with the existing investor agreements? (ii) would you accept any of these without negotiation? Try to do this without looking at the precedent first.

emails.subject: Knowledge update: Procurement Act 2023 - what's changed since 24 February
emails.body: Quarterly knowledge bulletin on the Procurement Act regime. Particularly relevant if you're doing public-sector commercial work. Headline: the new Procurement Review Unit has now issued its first three section 108 recommendations. Bulletin attached, full case digest in the knowledge bank.

emails.subject: CONFIDENTIAL: Cobalt drawdown - sign-off please
emails.body: Theo - Cobalt's first drawdown is tomorrow at 11:00. CP certificate attached. Please review against the conditions list and confirm you're satisfied. Marcus copied for visibility - this is a good example of what a clean drawdown looks like.

emails.subject: Mercer sponsorship - first draft from rugby club
emails.body: Joe here - Mercer's title-sponsorship draft from the club's solicitors is attached. Hannah wants Marcus involved as it's a stretch piece. Could you do a first read and flag the obvious commercial issues (term, exclusivity, IP, termination)? No urgent deadline yet.

emails.subject: PSL alert: Updated NDA precedent now in iManage
emails.body: We've published an updated mutual NDA precedent in iManage (corporate / NDAs / 2026). Key changes: (i) tightened definition of 'Confidential Information' to exclude reverse-engineerable subject matter, (ii) new clause on AI-tool restrictions, (iii) shortened return-or-destroy timetable. Please use this version for all new matters.

emails.subject: Last chance: register for LegalTech London 2026 (early-bird closes Friday)
emails.body: Hello Marcus, the early-bird rate for LegalTech London 2026 closes this Friday. Don't miss out on the largest gathering of legal innovators in Europe. Special trainee rate available. Click here to register.

emails.subject: Expenses query - taxi 04 May
emails.body: Marcus, your taxi expense from 04 May (£42.80) has been queried. Reason: receipt does not state the time of journey. Please re-upload a clearer receipt or confirm the time so we can validate against the late-working policy (after 21:30 only).

emails.subject: Brindle Foods - witness statement v3
emails.body: Imogen - latest version of the operations director's witness statement. Aaron's comments incorporated. Need a final clean version by Tuesday so we can send to the client for sign-off. Marcus copied for visibility - the witness-statement workflow is a useful one to know.

emails.subject: FYI - Fed rate decision implications memo
emails.body: Brief memo on yesterday's Fed decision and what it means for our facility-agreement work. Headline: floating-rate clients with USD tranches should expect sharper margin step-ups; our standard MAC and prepayment language is still robust. Long memo on the Banking knowledge page.

emails.subject: Mid-seat review - 22 May, 14:00
emails.body: Marcus - mid-seat review booked for 22 May at 14:00 in my office. Please come prepared with: (i) two pieces of work you're proud of, (ii) two areas where you want stretch, (iii) seat rotation preferences. Should take 45 min.

emails.subject: Cobalt decommissioning - reserve account structuring options
emails.body: Will / Theo - I want a one-page comparison of the three reserve-account structures we discussed: (a) escrow, (b) Letter of Credit, (c) parent-company guarantee. Pros / cons / typical client preference. Marcus copied if anyone has time to involve him - this is a good cross-practice piece.

emails.subject: Transform your DD process with [VENDOR] - free pilot for top 50 firms
emails.body: Hi Marcus, I came across your role at the firm and thought you'd be the perfect contact for our AI-powered DD platform. We're currently offering a free 90-day pilot to top 50 firms. Could I book a 15-minute call this week?

emails.subject: Saxon & Reed s166 - hearing prep
emails.body: Imogen - skilled-person interview is on the 21st. Please pull together: (i) chronology of the principal incidents, (ii) extracted communications between the relevant individuals, (iii) compliance-team contemporaneous notes. Marcus copied if you can use a hand.

emails.subject: Nairo Innovation Newsletter: April market signals
emails.body: April newsletter - market signals across legal AI, deal volume, regulatory changes, and ten things worth reading. Highlights this month: GenAI adoption among UK in-house teams jumps to 62%; ICO opens consultation on AI in employment; Tessera-style mid-market deal volume up 14% YoY.

emails.subject: Brindle breach - ICO follow-up letter received
emails.body: ICO has come back with five further questions following the initial 72-hour notification. Letter attached. Hannah - we should talk Friday morning before responding. Marcus - if you have capacity, draft the chronology section using the incident log; will pay back in good karma.

emails.subject: Quill influencer dispute - mediation prep
emails.body: Mediation booked for 16 June. Marcus - if you want exposure to a softer dispute resolution process, you're welcome to sit in. In the meantime, do you fancy doing a one-page summary of the strengths and weaknesses on each side? Useful for the position paper. Optional but valuable.

emails.subject: Quick research - earn-out reference accounts, half a page by 3pm?
emails.body: Marcus - I'm on the IC call at 4pm and Tessera want chapter and verse on the earn-out reference accounts. Can you do me half a page on (i) what 'reference accounts' typically means in a PE earn-out vs management accounts, (ii) whether the buyer or seller normally prepares them, and (iii) the usual dispute mechanism. Two or three precedents would be great if you can find them on iManage. By 3pm please. G.

emails.subject: NSI clean team precedent - do we have one?
emails.body: Marcus - Marston oncology deal is going to need a clean team protocol for commercially sensitive data because the target overlaps with one of Marston's existing licensed assets. Do we have a precedent in iManage? Christine in Knowledge would know. If not, ask her where the closest equivalent sits. No rush, end of week is fine, but it's a good thing for you to have a stab at drafting.

emails.subject: FT calling on dual-class shares - 200 words by 1pm?
emails.body: Marcus - the FT is doing a piece on dual-class share structures following the latest LSE consultation and asked me for a comment. I haven't got time to draft. Can you give me 200 words on (1) where we are on dual-class on the LSE post-reform, (2) why founders argue for them and why governance investors push back, (3) one line on what we're seeing on AIM. Christine on cc - please point Marcus at the firm's existing client briefing on this if there is one. By 1pm if possible.

emails.subject: Quick check - is 18-month escrow in line with market?
emails.body: Marcus - the buyers are pushing for a 24-month escrow on Lighthouse. George wants to push back at 18. Can you do a quick check across the AGM and Practical Law deal data - what's the median escrow period on UK private M&A in the last 24 months for a £100-250m deal? Two bullets is plenty. By tomorrow morning. Thanks.

emails.subject: AGNDA precedent - need the AI carve-out version
emails.body: Marcus - I need both (a) the latest AGNDA mutual NDA precedent and (b) the version with the AI training carve-out that Christine sent round last month. Could you dig them out of iManage and email both to me? Going into a call at 2pm with Lattice's GC and I want to be ready to send on the spot.

emails.subject: Sponsorship clause - just tell me what's wrong with this
emails.body:
Marcus - the rugby club have sent through their draft termination clause and something isn't right but I'm too deep in the IP indemnity to look at it properly. Can you read it (clause 17, copy below) and tell me in two bullets what's off? Don't redraft, just diagnose. Back to you in an hour or so.

---

'17.1 Either party may terminate this Agreement immediately on written notice if the other party suffers reputational damage. 17.2 The Club may terminate this Agreement at any time on three months' notice. 17.3 The Sponsor may terminate this Agreement only with the Club's prior written consent.'

emails.subject: Lighthouse CP tracker v8 - updated, please review
emails.body: George - CP tracker updated to v8 with status as at end of today. Six CPs now satisfied, three in progress, two yet to start. Antitrust filing has been accepted but no decision date yet. The pension trustee consent is the long pole - they're meeting on the 22nd. Marcus on cc - please pick up the warranty bring-down checklist tomorrow morning and ping me by lunchtime. Sarah.

emails.subject: Project Birch - signing checklist v3 (items 14-18 outstanding)
emails.body:
Sarah - latest signing checklist attached. Items 1-13 ticked. Outstanding:

14. Disclosure Letter (Wilkes & Frinton chasing - see separate email)
15. Board resolutions of BidCo (with the tax team for review of point 3)
16. NSI filing receipt (filed but no acknowledgement yet)
17. Director CVs for the W&I broker
18. Counterparty consent letters (3 of 5 received)

Marcus - if you can chase items 17 and 18 tomorrow morning that would be a help. Ella.

emails.subject: Cobalt closing agenda v4 - drawdown step added
emails.body: Daniel - closing agenda v4 attached. Added (1) the initial drawdown step under Facility A, (2) the tax clearance order before the security release, (3) a placeholder for the funds-flow confirmation from Northbank. Marcus on cc - please make sure your CP tracker mirrors items 4(a)-(g) of the agenda; we had a near-miss on Hawthorn because the two diverged. Will.

emails.subject: Helix - side letter tracker for Meridian and existing investors
emails.body: Marcus - can you start a side letter tracker for Helix? Format: investor, key ask, who is negotiating, status, residual risk. Meridian have asked for: (1) MFN, (2) co-sale waiver, (3) information rights at 3% instead of 5%. The Series A investors will likely ask for MFN through. Spreadsheet template on iManage in /Helix/Working/Trackers. Update at end of each day this week please.

emails.subject: RE: URGENT: Project Lighthouse - SPA mark-up needed by 9am tomorrow
emails.body:
Marcus - good draft, comments below. Don't redraft yet, talk to me at 10.

1. Clause 6.3 (warranty cap) - you've left it open ended. We agreed 50%, accept 75%. Put 50% on the page and a stet comment for the buyers.
2. Clause 6.4 (de minimis) - your £25k is too low for this deal. Push to £75k.
3. Clause 9.1 (limitation period) - 18 months for general warranties is fine, but you've extended Tax to 7 years which is correct, well done.
4. Clause 9.6 (fraud carve-out) - the buyer's wording extends to 'reckless misrepresentation'. Push back hard, that's not market.
5. Clause 11 (set-off) - you've accepted their full set-off rights against escrow. Limit to claims that are agreed or judgment-determined.
6. Schedule 4 (specific indemnities) - I want a specific on the Larchwood retailer dispute. Three lines, you can borrow from m005.

Good instinct on flagging the management warranty knowledge qualifiers. We need to push hardest on that. Call me when you're at your desk. G.

emails.subject: Birch DD summary memo - thoughts
emails.body:
Marcus - thanks for the draft. Some thoughts before you send to Tessera.

1. Your conclusion is buried in paragraph 14. Move it to the top - first paragraph should be the answer. 'We have identified three deal-stoppers and seven significant risks. Subject to those being addressed, the target is acquirable.' Then unpack.
2. The risk grading (red/amber/green) is fine but you haven't said what each colour means in dollar terms or process terms. Add a one-line key.
3. Cut the methodology paragraph entirely. Tessera don't care, they care about the answer.
4. The 'open issues' section is too long. If it's not actionable for IC, it doesn't belong in the memo. Make a separate annex.
5. Page 3 - the data protection commentary is technically correct but reads like a textbook. Three bullets: what's wrong, what it costs, how we fix.

On track. Send me the next version by Thursday lunchtime. Catherine.

emails.subject: RE: Lighthouse - board pack for Friday's board meeting
emails.body:
Marcus - draft is solid but needs surgery before George reads it tomorrow.

- Section 4 (commercial points) is too long. Drop to 3 bullets. George knows the detail, he wants the snapshot.
- Move the DD red-flag log to the top of the deck. The three deal-stoppers should be the first thing the board sees, not buried at page 11.
- The timetable slide - take out the swimlanes, replace with a 5-step gantt. The board members are non-lawyers, they will not read a swimlane.
- The 'open points' page reads like our internal worksheet. Rewrite as 'recommendations to the board' with a yes/no decision against each.
- The data protection point on page 9 - speak to Ines, she had a view I didn't fully understand. Get it landed cleanly.

If you can turn this around by 9am tomorrow we'll have time to review with George before it goes to the client. Sarah.

emails.subject: 1954 Act brief - your draft, my comments
emails.body:
Marcus - good first draft of the 1954 Act brief on the four Westgate units. Two structural points and four detail points.

Structural:
(i) You haven't told the client what to do. The brief should end with 'we recommend serving section 25 counter-notices on units B and D, and negotiating renewals on A and C'. As drafted it reads like a textbook chapter.
(ii) The 'ground (f)' redevelopment analysis on units B and D is good but you need to flag the risk that the landlord challenges intention. One paragraph on Pirelli Tyres / S Franses, not a treatise.

Detail:
- p3: the comparables you cite are from 2019. There's been movement since. Olivia can point you at the recent retail comparables in our database.
- p5: 'tenant friendly' is not legal language. Either say what you mean or take it out.
- p7: the break clause point assumes a particular construction of the lease that I don't think survives the 2023 case (see Hannah's note in iManage).
- p9: your interim rent calculation - you've used s24A. For these tenancies it's s24B. Check.

Good instinct on the holding-over risk. Resend by Thursday EOD please. Bea.

emails.subject: Project Lighthouse - SPA and Disclosure Letter for execution
emails.body:
Sasha, Rebecca - please find attached engrossed versions of (1) the SPA and (2) the Disclosure Letter for execution on Friday.

Could you arrange for the signatures of both directors on the SPA signature page, and Rebecca only on the Disclosure Letter. Counsel for the buyer will collect the originals at our offices on Friday at 11am. Please confirm the signing time works your end.

Marcus and Sarah on cc. Ella from our team will coordinate the closing logistics - any practical questions please go to her.

Many thanks for the work this week. We are in good shape. George.
